feature: Add list-specs CLI command for spec enumeration
description: Add a dedicated list-specs CLI command that enumerates all specs with their metadata including status, worktree info, and review state. Currently the status command only provides spec names without details.
workflow_rationale: This is a simple enhancement task. The list-specs command already exists in the legacy CLI (scripts/autoflow.py) and works correctly. The task is to: (1) verify the existing implementation works correctly, (2) enhance the modern Click CLI (autoflow/cli.py) to show the same detailed metadata, and (3) add comprehensive tests. No multi-phase workflow or service dependencies are needed.

phases.name: Verify Existing Implementation
phases.description: Verify that the existing list-specs command in the legacy CLI works correctly and outputs all required metadata fields (status, worktree, review state).

subtasks.description: Verify list-specs command outputs all required metadata

verification.command: python3 scripts/autoflow.py list-specs | python3 -c "import sys, json; data = json.load(sys.stdin); assert all('slug' in s and 'title' in s and 'status' in s and 'worktree' in s and 'review' in s for s in data), 'Missing required fields'; print('OK')"

subtasks.notes: The legacy CLI command at scripts/autoflow.py lines 5015-5038 already implements this feature. This task verifies it works correctly. Verification passed - all 2 specs have all required fields: slug, title, status, worktree, review.

phases.name: Enhance Modern Click CLI
phases.description: Enhance the spec list command in the modern Click CLI (autoflow/cli.py) to output detailed metadata including status, worktree info, and review state, matching the legacy CLI functionality.

subtasks.description: Add helper functions to load worktree and review metadata
subtasks.notes:
Added helper functions to autoflow/cli/utils.py and exported them from autoflow/cli/__init__.py:
- _get_worktree_metadata(spec_slug, config) -> dict with path, branch, base_branch
- _get_review_state_metadata(spec_slug, config) -> dict with approval state
- Supporting utilities: _slugify, _read_json_or_default, _spec_dir, _worktree_path, _worktree_branch, _detect_base_branch
Pattern follows scripts/autoflow.py normalize_worktree_metadata and load_review_state.
Verification passed: functions are importable from autoflow.cli package.

subtasks.description: Update spec list command to include detailed metadata

verification.command: python3 -m autoflow.cli spec list --output-json 2>&1 | python3 -c "import sys, json; data = json.load(sys.stdin); specs = data.get('specs', []); assert len(specs) > 0, 'No specs found'; assert all('status' in s and 'worktree' in s and 'review' in s for s in specs), 'Missing metadata fields'; print('OK')"

subtasks.notes:
Enhanced spec_list() function in autoflow/cli.py to:
- Read metadata from .autoflow/specs/*/metadata.json files
- Load review state from review_state.json files
- Return detailed metadata for each spec: slug, title, summary, status, created_at, updated_at, worktree, review
- Updated both JSON and human-readable output formats

Verification passed: Found 2 specs, all with required fields (status, worktree, review). The implementation matches the legacy CLI's list-specs functionality.

Committed as: b8784b8

phases.name: Add Comprehensive Tests
phases.description: Add comprehensive tests for the list-specs command to ensure it works correctly and handles edge cases.

subtasks.description: Add test for legacy CLI list-specs command
subtasks.notes:
Added TestLegacyCliListSpecs class with 4 comprehensive tests:
- test_list_specs_outputs_all_required_fields: Verifies all required fields (slug, title, summary, status, created_at, updated_at, worktree, review) with subfields (path, branch, base_branch, approved, approved_by, review_count)
- test_list_specs_with_empty_specs_dir: Verifies empty array returned when no specs exist
- test_list_specs_sorts_by_created_at_descending: Verifies specs sorted by created_at descending (newest first)
- test_list_specs_handles_missing_fields_gracefully: Verifies missing optional fields get default values

Verification passed: All 4 tests PASSED.

Committed as: eb3fe08

subtasks.description: Add test for modern CLI spec list command
subtasks.notes: Add tests to verify the modern CLI spec list command outputs all required metadata fields. Test both JSON and human-readable output formats.

parallel_groups.reason: Phase 1 verifies existing implementation, Phase 2 enhances modern CLI - they can run in parallel since they work on different codebases.

parallelism.speedup_estimate: 1.2x faster than sequential (minimal parallelism benefit due to dependencies)

acceptance_criteria: Legacy CLI list-specs command outputs all required metadata (status, worktree, review state)
Modern CLI spec list command outputs all required metadata (status, worktree, review state)
Both JSON and human-readable output formats work correctly

verification_steps.name: Verify Legacy CLI Command
verification_steps.command: python3 scripts/autoflow.py list-specs | python3 -c "import sys, json; data = json.load(sys.stdin); assert all('slug' in s and 'title' in s and 'status' in s and 'worktree' in s and 'review' in s for s in data)"
verification_steps.expected_outcome: All specs have required metadata fields

verification_steps.name: Verify Modern CLI Command
verification_steps.command: python3 -m autoflow.cli spec list --output-json | python3 -c "import sys, json; data = json.load(sys.stdin); specs = data.get('specs', []); assert all('status' in s and 'worktree' in s and 'review' in s for s in specs)"
verification_steps.expected_outcome: All specs have required metadata fields

verification_strategy.reasoning: Low risk change - adding metadata output to an existing command. The legacy CLI already has this functionality, so we're essentially porting it to the modern CLI and adding tests. Unit tests are sufficient to verify the functionality.

commands.expected_output: JSON array with specs containing slug, title, status, worktree, review fields
JSON object with specs array containing slug, title, status, worktree, review fields
